LAW AND MOTION, 302, INTERVENOR LISA BERGLUND, PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE OF THE ESTATE OF JOY COVEY'S MOTION TO INTERVENE AND TO STAY AND ABATE THIS ACTION DUE TO ANOTHER ACTION PENDING - THE COURT DENIES MS. BERGLUND'S MOTION TO INTERVENE AND STAY. FIRST, THE COURT DETERMINES THAT MS. BERGLUND'S INTERESTS, I.E., THE INTERESTS OF TYLER GERSTEIN, ARE ADEQUATELY REPRESENTED IN THIS ACTION AND INTERVENTION IS UNNECESSARY. SEE CCP 387(B). SECOND, EVEN IF THE COURT WERE TO PERMIT INTERVENTION, THE COURT FINDS THAT THIS ACTION SHOULD NOT BE STAYED UNDER THE DOCTRINE OF CONCURRENT EXCLUSIVE JURISDICTION GIVEN THE PARTICULAR CIRCUMSTANCES. THE DOCTRINE "SHOULD NOT BE A RIGID INFLEXIBLE PRINCIPLE, PARTICULARLY WHERE AS HERE, THE MULTIPLE LAWSUITS WERE BROUGHT ON BEHALF OF MINORS, FILED ESSENTIALLY SIMULTANEOUSLY, AND SERVED WITHIN A BRIEF PERIOD. UNDER THESE CIRCUMSTANCES, THERE IS NO PRINCIPLED BASIS FOR REQUIRING A COURT TO FAVOR ONE LAWSUIT OVER THE OTHER SOLELY BECAUSE OF THE TIMING OF THE LAWSUITS." WILLIAMS V. SUP. CRT. (2007) 147 CAL.APP.4TH 36, 53. IN THIS CASE, THE SAN MATEO AND SAN FRANCISCO ACTIONS WERE FILED AND SERVED ALMOST SIMULTANEOUSLY. THE COURT DETERMINES THAT ALLOWING THE SAN FRANCISCO WRONGFUL DEATH ACTION TO MOVE FORWARD IS BETTER AS THAT ACTION IS BEING PROSECUTED BY TYLER GERSTEIN'S CUSTODIAL FATHER. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976 (302)

MINI MINUTES FOR SEP-29-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT EXPRESS MESSENGER SYSTEMS, INC DBA ONTRAC'S MOTION FOR ORDER COORDINATING ACTIONS IS DENIED. THE RELIEF SOUGHT IS CONTRARY TO GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 955.2. THE "[N]OTWITHSTANDING ANY OTHER PROVISION OF THE LAW" LANGUAGE IN GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 955.2, "EXPRESSES LEGISLATIVE INTENT THAT THE STATUTE CONTROL VENUE IN ALL TORT CASES IN WHICH STATE IS A DEFENDANT EVEN THOUGH BY REASON OF JOINDER OF OTHER DEFENDANTS VENUE WOULD ALSO BE PROPER IN ANOTHER COUNTY." (SEE STATE V. SUPERIOR COURT OF LOS ANGELES COUNTY (1967) 252 CAL.APP.2D 637.) THE STATUTE CONFERS DISCRETION TO CHOOSE A DIFFERENT VENUE ONLY ON THE SUPERIOR COURT OF THE COUNTY WHERE THE INJURY OCCURRED. (STATE OF CALIFORNIA V. SUPERIOR COURT CLARK (1987) 193 CAL.APP.3D 328, 330.) BECAUSE THE INJURY WHICH PRODUCED THE ACCIDENT OCCURRED IN SAN MATEO COUNTY AND NOT IN SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY, THE SAN FRANCISCO SUPERIOR COURT SAN FRANCISCO SUPERIOR COURT IS NOT ENTITLED TO EXERCISE THE AUTHORITY PROVIDED IN GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 955.2. THUS, THE SAN FRANCISCO SUPERIOR COURT CANNOT ORDER THE COORDINATION OF THE BERGLAND CASE AND THE GERSTEIN CASE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: A. Petition To Approve Compromise Of Pending Action For Minor
Off Calendar May-12-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for a Hearing on a Minor's Compromise.
Department 514, Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: A. Mok, CSR: Mitch Lyon, CSR#6840.

Richard Henry Schoenberger, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff and Petitioner: LEE GERSTEIN,
M.D., father and guardian ad litem for Minor: TYLER GERSTEIN present in court.
Steven Toschi, Esq., and James Merriman, Esq., appearing for Defendant Express Messenger
Systems Inc. DBA Ontrac.
Michael D. Mutalipassi, Esq., appearing for Defendants Raheel Nadeem dba Shan Express and
Justin James Hanesworth Castellanos.
Witness Sheri Levitin appearing for Petitioner.
Petitioner and witness sworn and examined.

After testimony and consideration of the evidence presented, the Petition is granted and
compromise is approved as follows:
Total Settlement: $6,250,000.00
Attorney's Fees: $1,506,125.67 payable to Law Firm of Walkup, Melodia, Kelly & Schoenberger
Costs: $105,497.32
A net balance of $4,638,377.01 to be deposited into a single premium differed annuity at:
Berkshire Hathaway Life Insurance Company of NE. The annuity provides for payments to TYLER GERSTEIN of $25,000 on 9/30/2022; $5,325,000 on 9/30/2034, and $5,325,000 on 9/30/2044.

$149,248.23 will be transferred to the California Uniform Transfers to Minors Act account, for the sole purpose of enabling charitable giving by TYLER GERSTEIN. Annual distributions may not exceed $25,000.00, and funds may only be withdrawn with checks paid directly to 26 USC 501(c)(3) entities.

Counsel to submit Order Granting approval of Compromise of Disputed Claim of Minor for Court Signature.
Status Hearing Scheduled on August 18, 2016 at 9:00 AM, in Department 514 for filing of
receipt for the deposit of money into an annuity.
No appearance is required if the receipt is filed and courtesy copy lodged with Dept. 514
prior to Hearing.

Off Calendar Aug-27-2015 Continued To Feb-22-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion For Order Coordinating Actions
Off Calendar Sep-29-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT EXPRESS MESSENGER SYSTEMS, INC dba ONTRAC'S Motion For Order Coordinating Actions is denied. The relief sought is contrary to Government Code section 955.2. The "[n]otwithstanding any other provision of the law" language in Government Code section 955.2, "expresses legislative intent that the statute control venue in all tort cases in which state is a defendant even though by reason of joinder of other defendants venue would also be proper in another county." (See State v. Superior Court of Los Angeles County (1967) 252 Cal.App.2d 637.) The statute confers discretion to choose a different venue only on the superior court of the county where the injury occurred. (State of California v. Superior Court Clark (1987) 193 Cal.App.3d 328, 330.) Because the injury which produced the accident occurred in San Mateo County and not in San Francisco County, the San Francisco Superior Court San Francisco Superior Court is not entitled to exercise the authority provided in Government Code section 955.2. Thus, the San Francisco Superior Court cannot order the coordination of the Bergland case and the Gerstein case. Order signed. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ajr

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Intervene And To Stay And Abate This Action Due To Another Action Pending
Off Calendar Jun-16-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RICHARD H. SCHOENBERGER OF THE LAW OFFICES OF WALKUP, MELODIA, KELLY & SCHOENBERGER, TEL. NO. 981-7210 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JERMEY M. JESSUP OF THE LAW OFFICES OF CHOLAKIAN & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. (650) 871-9544 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT ON TRAC;
ATTY. MICHAEL S. DANKO OF THE DANKO & MEREDITH TRIAL LAWYERS, TEL. NO. (650) 453-3600 APPEARING FOR THE INTERVENOR BERGLUND;
ATTY. JOANNE MADDEN OF LECLAIR RYAN LLP, TEL. NO. 913-4912 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT EXPRESS MESSENGERS INC. BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; INTERVENOR LISA BERGLUND, PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE OF THE ESTATE OF JOY COVEY'S MOTION To Intervene And To Stay And Abate This Action Due To Another Action Pending - The court denies Ms. Berglund's Motion to Intervene and Stay. First, the court determines that Ms. Berglund's interests, i.e., the interests of Tyler Gerstein, are adequately represented in this action and intervention is unnecessary. See CCP 387(b). Second, even if the court were to permit intervention, the court finds that this action should not be stayed under the doctrine of concurrent exclusive jurisdiction given the particular circumstances. The doctrine "should not be a rigid inflexible principle, particularly where as here, the multiple lawsuits were brought on behalf of minors, filed essentially simultaneously, and served within a brief period. Under these circumstances, there is no principled basis for requiring a court to favor one lawsuit over the other solely because of the timing of the lawsuits." Williams v. Sup. Crt. (2007) 147 Cal.App.4th 36, 53. In this case, the San Mateo and San Francisco actions were filed and served almost simultaneously. The court determines that allowing the San Francisco wrongful death action to move forward is better as that action is being prosecuted by Tyler Gerstein's custodial father. Prevailing party to prepare form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg
